examples.en: The use of mobile phones has become very widespread throughout the country.

examples.es: Existe un descontento generalizado entre los empleados debido a los recientes recortes salariales.
examples.en: There is widespread discontent among the employees due to the recent salary cuts.

examples.es: La especie ha sufrido un declive generalizado en su población debido a la pérdida de su hábitat natural.
examples.en: The species has suffered a widespread decline in its population due to the loss of its natural habitat.

cards.example_sentence: The use of mobile phones has become very widespread throughout the country.
cards.example_es: El uso de los teléfonos móviles se ha vuelto muy generalizado en todo el país.
cards.example_en: The use of mobile phones has become very widespread throughout the country.
cards.mnemonic_en: A wide spread of general news blankets the town, like a generalizado blanket.

cards.definition_en: Being the most common, strongest, or most important element in a group or situation.

examples.es: El color azul es predominante en la decoración de la habitación.
examples.en: The color blue is predominant in the room's decor.

examples.es: El viento del norte es el factor predominante que afecta el clima de esta región costera.
examples.en: The north wind is the predominant factor affecting the climate of this coastal region.

examples.es: A pesar de la diversidad cultural, el inglés sigue siendo el idioma predominante en las reuniones de negocios internacionales.
examples.en: Despite cultural diversity, English remains the predominant language in international business meetings.

cards.example_sentence: The color blue is predominant in the room's decor.
cards.example_es: El color azul es predominante en la decoración de la habitación.
cards.example_en: The color blue is predominant in the room's decor.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a PRE-DOMINANT lion, the king before any other, just like a predominante leader.

cards.definition_en: Existing beneath the surface or forming the basis of something.

examples.es: El problema subyacente es la falta de comunicación.
examples.en: The underlying problem is the lack of communication.

examples.es: Debemos analizar las causas subyacentes de este conflicto económico.
examples.en: We must analyze the underlying causes of this economic conflict.

examples.es: Existe una tensión subyacente en la sala cada vez que mencionan el presupuesto.
examples.en: There is an underlying tension in the room whenever they mention the budget.

cards.example_sentence: The underlying problem is the lack of communication.
cards.example_es: El problema subyacente es la falta de comunicación.
cards.example_en: The underlying problem is the lack of communication.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a submarine (sub) hidden under the waves, the underlying secret.

cards.definition_en: To go beyond or rise above something, especially limits, expectations, or ordinary experience.

examples.es: El arte de Van Gogh logra trascender el tiempo y sigue inspirando a nuevas generaciones.
examples.en: Van Gogh's art manages to transcend time and continues to inspire new generations.

examples.es: Los principios éticos de la organización deben trascender las fronteras nacionales para garantizar la justicia global.
examples.en: The organization's ethical principles must transcend national borders to ensure global justice.

examples.es: Su dedicación al servicio comunitario logró trascender sus deberes laborales habituales.
examples.en: Her dedication to community service managed to transcend her regular job duties.

cards.example_sentence: Van Gogh's art manages to transcend time and continues to inspire new generations.
cards.example_es: El arte de Van Gogh logra trascender el tiempo y sigue inspirando a nuevas generaciones.
cards.example_en: Van Gogh's art manages to transcend time and continues to inspire new generations.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a ladder that trascends the clouds, letting you rise beyond all limits.

cards.definition_en: To weaken or damage something gradually or subtly.

examples.es: Las críticas constantes pueden perjudicar la confianza del equipo.
examples.en: Constant criticism can undermine the team's confidence.

examples.es: El escándalo financiero comenzó a perjudicar la reputación de la empresa ante sus inversores.
examples.en: The financial scandal began to undermine the company's reputation among its investors.

examples.es: No permitas que sus comentarios negativos lleguen a perjudicar tu determinación para alcanzar tus metas.
examples.en: Do not let their negative comments undermine your determination to reach your goals.

cards.example_sentence: Constant criticism can undermine the team's confidence.
cards.example_es: Las críticas constantes pueden perjudicar la confianza del equipo.
cards.example_en: Constant criticism can undermine the team's confidence.
cards.mnemonic_en: A miner digs under the bridge, causing it to collapse and perjudicar the village.

cards.definition_en: to act in accordance with a rule, request, or demand

examples.es: Es importante cumplir con las normas de seguridad.
examples.en: It is important to comply with the safety regulations.

examples.es: La empresa debe cumplir con los estándares ambientales establecidos por la ley.
examples.en: The company must comply with the environmental standards established by law.

examples.es: Si no logras cumplir con los requisitos del contrato, podrías enfrentar una demanda.
examples.en: If you fail to comply with the contract requirements, you could face a lawsuit.

cards.example_sentence: It is important to comply with the safety regulations.
cards.example_es: Es importante cumplir con las normas de seguridad.
cards.example_en: It is important to comply with the safety regulations.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a cumulative pile of rules you must comply with.

cards.definition_en: to follow or comply with a rule, guideline, or request

examples.es: Es importante ajustarse a las normas de la empresa.
examples.en: It is important to adhere to the company's rules.

examples.es: Todos los participantes deben ajustarse a los términos establecidos en el contrato.
examples.en: All participants must adhere to the terms established in the contract.

examples.es: El arquitecto tuvo que ajustarse a las estrictas regulaciones de seguridad del edificio.
examples.en: The architect had to adhere to the building's strict safety regulations.

cards.example_sentence: It is important to adhere to the company's rules.
cards.example_es: Es importante ajustarse a las normas de la empresa.
cards.example_en: It is important to adhere to the company's rules.
cards.mnemonic_en: A deer adjusts to the fence, just like you adhere to rules.

cards.definition_en: to cause something to happen or exist
collocations: give rise to a new trend

examples.es: La nueva política puede dar lugar a protestas masivas.
examples.en: The new policy can give rise to massive protests.

examples.es: El aumento de las temperaturas globales suele dar lugar a fenómenos meteorológicos extremos.
examples.en: Rising global temperatures often give rise to extreme weather events.

examples.es: La falta de comunicación clara en el equipo puede dar lugar a malentendidos costosos.
examples.en: A lack of clear communication within the team can give rise to costly misunderstandings.

cards.example_sentence: The new policy can give rise to massive protests.
cards.example_es: La nueva política puede dar lugar a protestas masivas.
cards.example_en: The new policy can give rise to massive protests.
cards.mnemonic_en: A river gives rise to a new path, just like "dar" creates space for something to happen.

cards.definition_en: To consider or regard something when making a decision.
collocations: take into account the fact that

examples.es: Debes tener en cuenta el clima antes de salir.
examples.en: You should take into account the weather before going out.

examples.es: Al planificar el presupuesto, hay que tener en cuenta los gastos imprevistos.
examples.en: When planning the budget, one must take into account the unexpected expenses.

examples.es: El comité decidió tener en cuenta todas las sugerencias de los empleados antes de votar.
examples.en: The committee decided to take into account all the employees' suggestions before voting.

cards.example_sentence: You should take into account the weather before going out.
cards.example_es: Debes tener en cuenta el clima antes de salir.
cards.example_en: You should take into account the weather before going out.
cards.mnemonic_en: Take a taco, then count the beans—cuenta reminds you to take into account.

cards.definition_en: to arrive at a final decision or judgment after considering the evidence
collocations: reach the conclusion that it is

examples.es: Para llegar a la conclusión de que el tratamiento funciona, los investigadores necesitan una muestra más grande.
examples.en: In order to reach the conclusion that the treatment works, researchers need a larger sample.

examples.es: El comité debe llegar a la conclusión por unanimidad antes de que se implementen cambios en las políticas.
examples.en: The committee must reach the conclusion unanimously before any policy changes are implemented.

examples.es: Solo después de examinar todas las pruebas puede el jurado llegar a la conclusión de que el acusado es culpable.
examples.en: Only after examining all the evidence can the jury reach the conclusion that the defendant is guilty.

cards.example_sentence: In order to reach the conclusion that the treatment works, researchers need a larger sample.
cards.example_es: Para llegar a la conclusión de que el tratamiento funciona, los investigadores necesitan una muestra más grande.
cards.example_en: In order to reach the conclusion that the treatment works, researchers need a larger sample.
cards.mnemonic_en: I reach out and grab the final 'conclusión' banner at the race finish.

cards.definition_en: It means that something is true or applies to a great degree.
collocations: It is true to a large extent
The impact is felt to a large extent
Results vary to a large extent

examples.es: El éxito del proyecto depende, en gran medida, de la colaboración del equipo.
examples.en: The success of the project depends, to a large extent, on the team's collaboration.

examples.es: La crisis económica actual se debe, en gran medida, a la falta de inversión en infraestructura.
examples.en: The current economic crisis is, to a large extent, due to the lack of investment in infrastructure.

examples.es: Su decisión de renunciar fue, en gran medida, motivada por el ambiente tóxico en la oficina.
examples.en: Her decision to resign was, to a large extent, motivated by the toxic environment in the office.

cards.example_sentence: The success of the project depends, to a large extent, on the team's collaboration.
cards.example_es: El éxito del proyecto depende, en gran medida, de la colaboración del equipo.
cards.example_en: The success of the project depends, to a large extent, on the team's collaboration.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a huge tent covering a big measure of land

cards.definition_en: During a brief period of time, referring to the near future.
collocations: In the short term, we expect...
In the short term, the company will...
In the short term, this will improve

examples.es: A corto plazo, la empresa planea aumentar sus ventas en un 10%.
examples.en: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.

examples.es: A corto plazo, necesitamos reducir los gastos operativos para evitar un déficit presupuestario.
examples.en: In the short term, we need to cut operating expenses to avoid a budget deficit.

examples.es: Aunque el proyecto es ambicioso, a corto plazo nos centraremos únicamente en la fase de desarrollo inicial.
examples.en: Although the project is ambitious, in the short term we will focus solely on the initial development phase.

cards.example_sentence: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.
cards.example_es: A corto plazo, la empresa planea aumentar sus ventas en un 10%.
cards.example_en: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a short "corto" rope that snaps quickly, just like the short term.

cards.definition_en: refers to what will happen over a long period of time
collocations: In the long run, it will pay off
In the long run, the benefits outweigh the costs
In the long run, we see improvement

examples.es: A largo plazo, la inversión en educación reducirá la desigualdad.
examples.en: In the long run, investment in education will reduce inequality.

examples.es: Aunque el coche eléctrico es más caro ahora, a largo plazo ahorrarás mucho dinero en combustible.
examples.en: Although the electric car is more expensive now, in the long run you will save a lot of money on fuel.

examples.es: Debemos considerar las consecuencias ambientales de este proyecto a largo plazo antes de comenzar la construcción.
examples.en: We must consider the environmental consequences of this project in the long run before beginning construction.

cards.example_sentence: In the long run, investment in education will reduce inequality.
cards.example_es: A largo plazo, la inversión en educación reducirá la desigualdad.
cards.example_en: In the long run, investment in education will reduce inequality.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a long (largo) race that stretches far into the future.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something is being considered from one's own point of view.
collocations: From my perspective it seems that ...
From my perspective we can see ...

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, la propuesta de reforma laboral es demasiado arriesgada para la economía actual.
examples.en: From my perspective, the labor reform proposal is far too risky for the current economy.

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, el diseño minimalista de este edificio mejora significativamente la eficiencia energética.
examples.en: From my perspective, the minimalist design of this building significantly improves energy efficiency.

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, los avances en inteligencia artificial transformarán radicalmente el mercado laboral en la próxima década.
examples.en: From my perspective, advancements in artificial intelligence will radically transform the job market over the next decade.

cards.example_sentence: From my perspective, the labor reform proposal is far too risky for the current economy.
cards.example_es: Desde mi punto de vista, la propuesta de reforma laboral es demasiado arriesgada para la economía actual.
cards.example_en: From my perspective, the labor reform proposal is far too risky for the current economy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a point (punto) on a map, and you view it from your own perspective.

cards.definition_en: In the end, after everything else has been considered.

examples.es: En última instancia, la decisión de aceptar el nuevo puesto de trabajo depende únicamente de ti.
examples.en: Ultimately, the decision to accept the new job offer rests entirely with you.

examples.es: Aunque discutimos varios planes, en última instancia optamos por la opción más económica.
examples.en: Although we discussed several plans, we ultimately opted for the most budget-friendly option.

examples.es: El éxito de este proyecto depende, en última instancia, de nuestra capacidad para trabajar en equipo bajo presión.
examples.en: The success of this project ultimately depends on our ability to work as a team under pressure.

cards.example_sentence: Ultimately, the decision to accept the new job offer rests entirely with you.
cards.example_es: En última instancia, la decisión de aceptar el nuevo puesto de trabajo depende únicamente de ti.
cards.example_en: Ultimately, the decision to accept the new job offer rests entirely with you.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the final 'última' page flipping, revealing the ultimate outcome.

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate that something is being considered or discussed in relation to a particular subject.
main_translations_es: En lo que se refiere a
collocations: as far as the matter is concerned
as far as the law is concerned

examples.es: En lo que respecta a ..., la aerolínea seguirá ofreciendo equipaje facturado gratuito en los vuelos internacionales, aunque las tarifas de equipaje para trayectos domésticos permanecen sin cambios.
examples.en: As far as ... is concerned, the airline will continue offering free checked bags on international flights, although the baggage fees for domestic routes remain unchanged.

examples.es: En lo que respecta a ..., el departamento de recursos humanos aclaró que los empleados deberán presentarse en la oficina al menos dos días por semana para mantener su elegibilidad.
examples.en: As far as ... is concerned, the human resources department clarified that employees must come into the office at least two days per week to maintain their eligibility.

examples.es: En lo que respecta a ..., el director del proyecto anunció que cualquier retraso superior a 48 horas requerirá una justificación por escrito firmada por el supervisor del equipo.
examples.en: As far as ... is concerned, the project director announced that any delay exceeding 48 hours will require a written justification signed by the team supervisor.

cards.example_sentence: As far as ... is concerned, the airline will continue offering free checked bags on international flights, although the baggage fees for domestic routes remain unchanged.
cards.example_es: En lo que respecta a ..., la aerolínea seguirá ofreciendo equipaje facturado gratuito en los vuelos internacionales, aunque las tarifas de equipaje para trayectos domésticos permanecen sin cambios.
cards.example_en: As far as ... is concerned, the airline will continue offering free checked bags on international flights, although the baggage fees for domestic routes remain unchanged.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a faraway A (as) looking concerned at a sign reading en lo que respecta a.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something should be emphasized or remembered.
collocations: It is worth noting the following...
It is worth noting the fact that...

examples.es: Cabe destacar que la investigación reveló nuevos hallazgos sobre el clima.
examples.en: It is worth noting that the research revealed new findings about the climate.

examples.es: Cabe destacar que el candidato tiene una experiencia excepcional en gestión de crisis internacionales.
examples.en: It is worth noting that the candidate has exceptional experience in international crisis management.

examples.es: Cabe destacar que las ventas del último trimestre superaron todas las expectativas de los analistas financieros.
examples.en: It is worth noting that the last quarter's sales exceeded all the financial analysts' expectations.

cards.example_sentence: It is worth noting that the research revealed new findings about the climate.
cards.example_es: Cabe destacar que la investigación reveló nuevos hallazgos sobre el clima.
cards.example_en: It is worth noting that the research revealed new findings about the climate.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cape (cabe) that highlights a worth‑noting treasure.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something should be highlighted or mentioned.
collocations: It is worth pointing out that...
It is worth pointing out the fact that...
It is worth pointing out the importance of...

examples.es: Vale la pena señalar que el informe contiene varios errores.
examples.en: It is worth pointing out that the report contains several errors.

examples.es: Vale la pena señalar que los precios de las viviendas han subido drásticamente este trimestre.
examples.en: It is worth pointing out that housing prices have risen drastically this quarter.

examples.es: Vale la pena señalar que el candidato no tiene experiencia previa en el sector tecnológico.
examples.en: It is worth pointing out that the candidate lacks prior experience in the tech sector.

cards.example_sentence: It is worth pointing out that the report contains several errors.
cards.example_es: Vale la pena señalar que el informe contiene varios errores.
cards.example_en: It is worth pointing out that the report contains several errors.
cards.mnemonic_en: A valuable pen points out the secret.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something is certain and unquestionable.
collocations: There is no doubt about it.

examples.es: No cabe duda de que la nueva política mejorará la economía del país.
examples.en: There is no doubt that the new policy will improve the country's economy.

examples.es: No cabe duda de que el equipo de investigación ha descubierto una cura revolucionaria para esta enfermedad.
examples.en: There is no doubt that the research team has discovered a revolutionary cure for this disease.

examples.es: No cabe duda de que ella es la candidata más cualificada para asumir el puesto de directora ejecutiva.
examples.en: There is no doubt that she is the most qualified candidate to take on the role of chief executive officer.

cards.example_sentence: There is no doubt that the new policy will improve the country's economy.
cards.example_es: No cabe duda de que la nueva política mejorará la economía del país.
cards.example_en: There is no doubt that the new policy will improve the country's economy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cabbage (cabe) with the doubt question mark crossed out—no doubt.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to state that something is clearly apparent or obvious.
collocations: It is evident that the data shows...
It is evident that there is a problem
It is evident that this is the case

examples.es: Resulta evidente que la contaminación del aire está empeorando la salud pública.
examples.en: It is evident that air pollution is worsening public health.

examples.es: Resulta evidente que el equipo no ha practicado lo suficiente para ganar el campeonato.
examples.en: It is evident that the team has not practiced enough to win the championship.

examples.es: Resulta evidente que las nuevas políticas económicas han provocado un aumento en el desempleo.
examples.en: It is evident that the new economic policies have caused an increase in unemployment.

cards.example_sentence: It is evident that air pollution is worsening public health.
cards.example_es: Resulta evidente que la contaminación del aire está empeorando la salud pública.
cards.example_en: It is evident that air pollution is worsening public health.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a neon sign flashing EVIDENT above a Spanish street, shouting evidente for everyone to see.

cards.definition_en: A phrase indicating that something does not affect or limit another matter.
collocations: without prejudice to the rights of

examples.es: Sin perjuicio de lo acordado, la empresa podrá modificar los horarios.
examples.en: Without prejudice to what has been agreed, the company may change the working hours.

examples.es: Sin perjuicio de las nuevas normativas, los contratos vigentes seguirán siendo válidos.
examples.en: Without prejudice to the new regulations, the existing contracts will remain valid.

examples.es: Sin perjuicio de su derecho a apelar, el acusado debe cumplir la sentencia provisionalmente.
examples.en: Without prejudice to his right to appeal, the defendant must comply with the sentence provisionally.

cards.example_sentence: Without prejudice to what has been agreed, the company may change the working hours.
cards.example_es: Sin perjuicio de lo acordado, la empresa podrá modificar los horarios.
cards.example_en: Without prejudice to what has been agreed, the company may change the working hours.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sinless judge clearing the record: without prejudice.

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate that something is done in order to achieve or benefit something else.

examples.es: En aras de la seguridad, cerraron la carretera durante la tormenta.
examples.en: For the sake of safety, they closed the road during the storm.

examples.es: El gobierno redujo los impuestos en aras de estimular la economía local.
examples.en: The government lowered taxes for the sake of stimulating the local economy.

examples.es: Ella decidió perdonar a su hermano en aras de mantener la armonía familiar.
examples.en: She decided to forgive her brother for the sake of maintaining family harmony.

cards.example_sentence: For the sake of safety, they closed the road during the storm.
cards.example_es: En aras de la seguridad, cerraron la carretera durante la tormenta.
cards.example_en: For the sake of safety, they closed the road during the storm.
cards.mnemonic_en: Run a race for the sake of victory.

cards.definition_en: As a result; for that reason.

examples.es: Estaba lloviendo mucho, de ahí que cancelaron el partido.
examples.en: It was raining heavily, hence they cancelled the match.

examples.es: El candidato carecía de experiencia previa, de ahí que no lo contrataran para el puesto directivo.
examples.en: The candidate lacked previous experience, hence they did not hire him for the management position.

examples.es: La empresa ha registrado pérdidas millonarias este trimestre, de ahí que hayan decidido cerrar varias sucursales.
examples.en: The company has recorded million-dollar losses this quarter, hence they have decided to close several branches.

cards.example_sentence: It was raining heavily, hence they cancelled the match.
cards.example_es: Estaba lloviendo mucho, de ahí que cancelaron el partido.
cards.example_en: It was raining heavily, hence they cancelled the match.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hen seeing a clue, so from that (hence) you know the answer.

cards.definition_en: Considering that; used to introduce a reason or condition.
collocations: Given that we have no choice
Given that the data is correct

examples.es: Dado que el presupuesto ha sido recortado, debemos reducir los gastos de viaje.
examples.en: Given that the budget has been cut, we must reduce travel expenses.

examples.es: Dado que los resultados del experimento fueron concluyentes, el equipo decidió publicar el informe final.
examples.en: Given that the experiment results were conclusive, the team decided to publish the final report.

examples.es: Dado que no hay suficientes asientos en el auditorio, tendremos que transmitir la conferencia en línea.
examples.en: Given that there are not enough seats in the auditorium, we will have to stream the lecture online.

cards.example_sentence: Given that the budget has been cut, we must reduce travel expenses.
cards.example_es: Dado que el presupuesto ha sido recortado, debemos reducir los gastos de viaje.
cards.example_en: Given that the budget has been cut, we must reduce travel expenses.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture giving a dice called dado to someone, so you’re given that.

cards.definition_en: It means 'although' or 'even though'.

examples.es: El atleta terminó la maratón, aunque visiblemente agotado por el esfuerzo.
examples.en: The athlete finished the marathon, albeit visibly exhausted from the effort.

examples.es: La reunión fue productiva, aunque un poco caótica.
examples.en: The meeting was productive, albeit a bit chaotic.

examples.es: El nuevo sistema es eficiente, aunque bastante costoso de implementar.
examples.en: The new system is efficient, albeit quite expensive to implement.

cards.example_sentence: The athlete finished the marathon, albeit visibly exhausted from the effort.
cards.example_es: El atleta terminó la maratón, aunque visiblemente agotado por el esfuerzo.
cards.example_en: The athlete finished the marathon, albeit visibly exhausted from the effort.
cards.mnemonic_en: Albeit sounds like Spanish aunque, picture a tiny al bridge letting a though pass.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a contrast or comparison between two statements.

examples.es: Yo prefiero el té, mientras que mi hermano prefiere el café.
examples.en: I prefer tea, whereas my brother prefers coffee.

examples.es: El contrato estipula una jornada completa, mientras que el acuerdo verbal sugería un horario flexible.
examples.en: The contract stipulates a full-time schedule, whereas the verbal agreement suggested a flexible timetable.

examples.es: La empresa registró beneficios récord este trimestre, mientras que sus competidores directos sufrieron pérdidas significativas.
examples.en: The company reported record profits this quarter, whereas its direct competitors suffered significant losses.

cards.example_sentence: I prefer tea, whereas my brother prefers coffee.
cards.example_es: Yo prefiero el té, mientras que mi hermano prefiere el café.
cards.example_en: I prefer tea, whereas my brother prefers coffee.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sign saying WHERE next to a WHILE (mientras) sign, showing contrast – that's WHEREAS.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a statement that is different from or opposite to what has just been mentioned.

examples.es: El verano pasado fue extremadamente caluroso; en cambio, este año las temperaturas han sido inusualmente frescas.
examples.en: Last summer was extremely hot; by contrast, this year the temperatures have been unusually cool.

examples.es: La mayoría de los empleados optaron por trabajar desde casa; en cambio, ella prefirió seguir acudiendo a la oficina todos los días.
examples.en: Most employees opted to work from home; by contrast, she preferred to keep going into the office every day.

examples.es: El primer candidato presentó un plan de austeridad muy estricto; en cambio, su oponente propuso aumentar el gasto público para estimular la economía.
examples.en: The first candidate presented a very strict austerity plan; by contrast, his opponent proposed increasing public spending to stimulate the economy.

cards.example_sentence: Last summer was extremely hot; by contrast, this year the temperatures have been unusually cool.
cards.example_es: El verano pasado fue extremadamente caluroso; en cambio, este año las temperaturas han sido inusualmente frescas.
cards.example_en: Last summer was extremely hot; by contrast, this year the temperatures have been unusually cool.
cards.mnemonic_en: By contrast the picture flips like a cambio in a comic

cards.definition_en: In the same way or manner; similarly.

examples.es: El estudio confirma que el ejercicio regular reduce el estrés y, asimismo, mejora la calidad del sueño.
examples.en: The study confirms that regular exercise reduces stress and, likewise, improves sleep quality.

examples.es: El nuevo sistema mejora la seguridad de los datos y, asimismo, optimiza la velocidad de procesamiento del servidor.
examples.en: The new system improves data security and, likewise, optimizes the server's processing speed.

examples.es: El candidato prometió reducir los impuestos y, asimismo, se comprometió a mejorar la infraestructura pública.
examples.en: The candidate promised to lower taxes and, likewise, pledged to improve public infrastructure.

cards.example_sentence: The study confirms that regular exercise reduces stress and, likewise, improves sleep quality.
cards.example_es: El estudio confirma que el ejercicio regular reduce el estrés y, asimismo, mejora la calidad del sueño.
cards.example_en: The study confirms that regular exercise reduces stress and, likewise, improves sleep quality.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a twin saying likewise, holding a banner that reads asimismo.

cards.definition_en: In a way that indicates a result or effect; therefore.

examples.es: Por consiguiente, el proyecto se retrasó una semana.
examples.en: Consequently, the project was delayed by a week.

examples.es: No hubo suficientes entradas vendidas; por consiguiente, el concierto fue cancelado.
examples.en: There were not enough tickets sold; consequently, the concert was cancelled.

examples.es: El atleta sufrió una lesión grave y, por consiguiente, no pudo participar en los Juegos Olímpicos.
examples.en: The athlete suffered a serious injury and, consequently, could not participate in the Olympic Games.

cards.example_sentence: Consequently, the project was delayed by a week.
cards.example_es: Por consiguiente, el proyecto se retrasó una semana.
cards.example_en: Consequently, the project was delayed by a week.
cards.mnemonic_en: The word CONSEQUENTLY starts with cons-, reminding me of por consiguiente.

cards.definition_en: in spite of that; however

examples.es: No obstante, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
examples.en: Nevertheless, he decided to continue with the project.

examples.es: El pronóstico anunciaba tormentas fuertes; no obstante, el partido de fútbol se jugó según lo previsto.
examples.en: The forecast predicted heavy storms; nevertheless, the soccer match went ahead as scheduled.

examples.es: La empresa ha tenido pérdidas económicas este trimestre; no obstante, planean expandirse a nuevos mercados internacionales.
examples.en: The company has suffered financial losses this quarter; nevertheless, they plan to expand into new international markets.

cards.example_sentence: Nevertheless, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.example_es: No obstante, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
cards.example_en: Nevertheless, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture no obst(acle) ahead, so you say nevertheless and keep moving forward.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or qualifies what has just been said.

examples.es: Aun así, decidió ir al concierto a pesar de la lluvia.
examples.en: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.

examples.es: El proyecto tenía muchos errores técnicos; aun así, el equipo logró terminarlo a tiempo.
examples.en: The project had many technical flaws; even so, the team managed to finish it on time.

examples.es: Ella sabía que el examen sería extremadamente difícil, pero aun así estudió toda la noche.
examples.en: She knew the exam would be extremely difficult, but even so, she studied all night.

cards.example_sentence: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.
cards.example_es: Aun así, decidió ir al concierto a pesar de la lluvia.
cards.example_en: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize an even number holding a so sign, echoing aun así.

cards.definition_en: To emphasize or make something stand out.

examples.es: El informe pone de relieve la importancia de la educación temprana.
examples.en: The report serves to highlight the importance of early education.

examples.es: La investigación busca poner de relieve las discrepancias en los datos financieros del último trimestre.
examples.en: The investigation aims to highlight the discrepancies in the financial data from the last quarter.

examples.es: El uso de colores brillantes en el cartel ayuda a poner de relieve el mensaje principal de la campaña.
examples.en: The use of bright colors on the poster helps to highlight the main message of the campaign.

cards.example_sentence: The report serves to highlight the importance of early education.
cards.example_es: El informe pone de relieve la importancia de la educación temprana.
cards.example_en: The report serves to highlight the importance of early education.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a high lighthouse lighting a relief map, bringing the details into view.

cards.definition_en: to express doubt about the truth or validity of something.

examples.es: El comité decidió poner en tela de juicio la validez del informe.
examples.en: The committee decided to call into question the validity of the report.

examples.es: Los resultados inesperados del experimento obligaron a los científicos a poner en tela de juicio su hipótesis inicial.
examples.en: The unexpected results of the experiment forced the scientists to call into question their initial hypothesis.

examples.es: Su comportamiento errático durante la reunión hizo que todos empezaran a poner en tela de juicio su capacidad para liderar el proyecto.
examples.en: His erratic behavior during the meeting made everyone begin to call into question his ability to lead the project.

cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to call into question the validity of the report.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió poner en tela de juicio la validez del informe.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to call into question the validity of the report.
cards.mnemonic_en: I call the judge, asking if his fabric is questionable.

cards.definition_en: to take advantage of something for one's benefit

examples.es: La empresa supo sacar provecho de la nueva normativa para reducir sus costos.
examples.en: The company knew how to capitalize on the new regulations to cut its costs.

examples.es: El tenista profesional logró sacar provecho de la lesión de su oponente para ganar el partido.
examples.en: The professional tennis player managed to capitalize on his opponent's injury to win the match.

examples.es: Debemos sacar provecho de esta oportunidad única antes de que el mercado cambie por completo.
examples.en: We must capitalize on this unique opportunity before the market changes completely.

cards.example_sentence: The company knew how to capitalize on the new regulations to cut its costs.
cards.example_es: La empresa supo sacar provecho de la nueva normativa para reducir sus costos.
cards.example_en: The company knew how to capitalize on the new regulations to cut its costs.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cap pulling out (sacar) cash from a piggy bank, showing you capitalize on profit.

cards.definition_en: to deal effectively with something difficult or challenging

examples.es: Es difícil hacer frente a la pérdida de un ser querido.
examples.en: It's hard to cope with the loss of a loved one.

examples.es: Los empleados deben hacer frente a una carga de trabajo excesiva durante la temporada alta.
examples.en: Employees must cope with an excessive workload during the peak season.

examples.es: Ella aprendió a hacer frente a situaciones de estrés extremo manteniendo la calma.
examples.en: She learned to cope with extreme stress situations by staying calm.

cards.example_sentence: It's hard to cope with the loss of a loved one.
cards.example_es: Es difícil hacer frente a la pérdida de un ser querido.
cards.example_en: It's hard to cope with the loss of a loved one.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a cup (copa) placed right in front (frente) of you to help you cope.

cards.definition_en: To accept or assume accountability for something.

examples.es: Es importante asumir la responsabilidad de tus actos.
examples.en: It is important to take responsibility for your actions.

examples.es: Como gerente del proyecto, debes asumir la responsabilidad si el equipo no cumple con los plazos.
examples.en: As the project manager, you must take responsibility if the team misses the deadlines.

examples.es: El capitán decidió asumir la responsabilidad por el error de navegación que causó el accidente.
examples.en: The captain decided to take responsibility for the navigation error that caused the accident.

cards.example_sentence: It is important to take responsibility for your actions.
cards.example_es: Es importante asumir la responsabilidad de tus actos.
cards.example_en: It is important to take responsibility for your actions.
cards.mnemonic_en: Take a taco and carry the huge RESPONSIBILITY banner, just like asumir la responsabilidad.

cards.definition_en: To come to a mutual understanding or settlement.

examples.es: Después de largas negociaciones, finalmente logramos llegar a un acuerdo.
examples.en: After lengthy negotiations, we finally managed to reach an agreement.

examples.es: Los sindicatos y la dirección se reunieron toda la noche para intentar llegar a un acuerdo sobre los nuevos salarios.
examples.en: The unions and management met all night to try to reach an agreement regarding the new wages.

examples.es: Si no conseguimos llegar a un acuerdo antes de la fecha límite, el proyecto será cancelado inmediatamente.
examples.en: If we fail to reach an agreement before the deadline, the project will be cancelled immediately.

cards.example_sentence: After lengthy negotiations, we finally managed to reach an agreement.
cards.example_es: Después de largas negociaciones, finalmente logramos llegar a un acuerdo.
cards.example_en: After lengthy negotiations, we finally managed to reach an agreement.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hand reaching to seal an accord, a firm handshake of agreement.

cards.definition_en: To do something in order to achieve a result.

examples.es: Es hora de tomar medidas para reducir la contaminación.
examples.en: It's time to take action to reduce pollution.

examples.es: El gobierno decidió tomar medidas urgentes ante la crisis económica.
examples.en: The government decided to take action in response to the economic crisis.

examples.es: Debemos tomar medidas legales contra la empresa por incumplimiento de contrato.
examples.en: We must take action against the company for breach of contract.

cards.example_sentence: It's time to take action to reduce pollution.
cards.example_es: Es hora de tomar medidas para reducir la contaminación.
cards.example_en: It's time to take action to reduce pollution.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a tomato measuring with a ruler, then taking action.

cards.definition_en: To give priority to something, treating it as more important than other things.

examples.es: Debemos dar prioridad a la educación en nuestro presupuesto.
examples.en: We must prioritize education in our budget.

examples.es: El equipo médico decidió dar prioridad a los pacientes con heridas más graves tras el accidente.
examples.en: The medical team decided to prioritize patients with the most severe injuries after the accident.

examples.es: Para cumplir con la fecha de entrega, el gerente nos pidió dar prioridad a las tareas más urgentes del proyecto.
examples.en: To meet the deadline, the manager asked us to prioritize the most urgent tasks of the project.

cards.example_sentence: We must prioritize education in our budget.
cards.example_es: Debemos dar prioridad a la educación en nuestro presupuesto.
cards.example_en: We must prioritize education in our budget.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a PRIORITY stamp on a prize, showing you give it first place.

cards.definition_en: To fail to notice or consider something.

examples.es: El editor decidió pasar por alto el pequeño error tipográfico en la última página del libro.
examples.en: The editor decided to overlook the minor typo on the last page of the book.

examples.es: No puedes pasar por alto las señales de advertencia cuando el motor empieza a sobrecalentarse.
examples.en: You cannot overlook the warning signs when the engine starts to overheat.

examples.es: Es fácil pasar por alto los detalles importantes cuando estás bajo mucha presión en el trabajo.
examples.en: It is easy to overlook important details when you are under a lot of pressure at work.

cards.example_sentence: The editor decided to overlook the minor typo on the last page of the book.
cards.example_es: El editor decidió pasar por alto el pequeño error tipográfico en la última página del libro.
cards.example_en: The editor decided to overlook the minor typo on the last page of the book.
cards.mnemonic_en: I stand on a high overlook and watch a passing pasar train go by.

cards.definition_en: To act against something in order to reduce or neutralize its effect.

examples.es: El nuevo medicamento ayuda a contrarrestar los efectos secundarios del tratamiento.
examples.en: The new drug helps to counteract the side effects of the treatment.

examples.es: Es necesario implementar políticas fiscales para contrarrestar la inflación galopante en el país.
examples.en: It is necessary to implement fiscal policies to counteract the galloping inflation in the country.

examples.es: Los investigadores están buscando formas de contrarrestar el impacto negativo de la contaminación en los ecosistemas locales.
examples.en: Researchers are looking for ways to counteract the negative impact of pollution on local ecosystems.

cards.example_sentence: The new drug helps to counteract the side effects of the treatment.
cards.example_es: El nuevo medicamento ayuda a contrarrestar los efectos secundarios del tratamiento.
cards.example_en: The new drug helps to counteract the side effects of the treatment.
cards.mnemonic_en: A counter acts against a spill, so it counteracts the mess.

cards.definition_en: To strengthen or support something, often by adding material or effort.

examples.es: El entrenador decidió reforzar la defensa del equipo antes del próximo partido.
examples.en: The coach decided to reinforce the team's defense before the next match.

examples.es: Es necesario reforzar los cimientos del edificio para que pueda soportar el peso de los nuevos pisos.
examples.en: It is necessary to reinforce the building's foundations so it can support the weight of the new floors.

examples.es: El profesor utilizó ejemplos prácticos para reforzar los conceptos teóricos explicados en la clase de física.
examples.en: The teacher used practical examples to reinforce the theoretical concepts explained in the physics class.

cards.example_sentence: The coach decided to reinforce the team's defense before the next match.
cards.example_es: El entrenador decidió reforzar la defensa del equipo antes del próximo partido.
cards.example_en: The coach decided to reinforce the team's defense before the next match.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a REINFORCEing robot REINFORCING a wall, like a Spanish REFORZAR.

cards.definition_en: To make something less strong, less effective, or less able to resist.

examples.es: El frío intenso comenzó a debilitar las plantas del jardín.
examples.en: The intense cold began to weaken the garden's plants.

examples.es: La falta de inversión constante terminará por debilitar la infraestructura del puente.
examples.en: The lack of constant investment will eventually weaken the bridge's infrastructure.

examples.es: Los escándalos de corrupción suelen debilitar la confianza de los ciudadanos en sus líderes.
examples.en: Corruption scandals tend to weaken the citizens' trust in their leaders.

cards.example_sentence: The intense cold began to weaken the garden's plants.
cards.example_es: El frío intenso comenzó a debilitar las plantas del jardín.
cards.example_en: The intense cold began to weaken the garden's plants.
cards.mnemonic_en: A weak rope debil snaps, making the bridge weaken

cards.definition_en: to cause something to happen or to start a process.

examples.es: El descubrimiento de la nueva partícula podría desencadenar una revolución en la física.
examples.en: The discovery of the new particle could trigger a revolution in physics.

examples.es: Un comentario desafortunado en la reunión fue suficiente para desencadenar una discusión acalorada entre los socios.
examples.en: An unfortunate comment at the meeting was enough to trigger a heated argument between the partners.

examples.es: Ciertos alérgenos en el aire pueden desencadenar un ataque de asma severo en pacientes sensibles.
examples.en: Certain allergens in the air can trigger a severe asthma attack in sensitive patients.

cards.example_sentence: The discovery of the new particle could trigger a revolution in physics.
cards.example_es: El descubrimiento de la nueva partícula podría desencadenar una revolución en la física.
cards.example_en: The discovery of the new particle could trigger a revolution in physics.
cards.mnemonic_en: Pull the trigger and watch the chain snap, desencadenar the action.

cards.definition_en: To expect or look forward to something and act in advance based on that expectation.

examples.es: Es importante prever los problemas antes de que ocurran.
examples.en: It's important to anticipate problems before they happen.

examples.es: El equipo debe prever un aumento en la demanda durante la temporada navideña.
examples.en: The team must anticipate an increase in demand during the holiday season.

examples.es: Los arquitectos lograron prever las dificultades estructurales del edificio mediante simulaciones digitales.
examples.en: The architects managed to anticipate the building's structural difficulties through digital simulations.

cards.example_sentence: It's important to anticipate problems before they happen.
cards.example_es: Es importante prever los problemas antes de que ocurran.
cards.example_en: It's important to anticipate problems before they happen.
cards.mnemonic_en: I anticipate the test by pre-veer my study notes ahead

cards.definition_en: A successful result or accomplishment that someone has attained.

examples.es: Este es mi mayor logro hasta ahora.
examples.en: This is my greatest achievement so far.

examples.es: Recibir el premio Nobel fue un logro impresionante para la científica.
examples.en: Receiving the Nobel Prize was an impressive achievement for the scientist.

examples.es: Completar el maratón después de meses de entrenamiento fue un logro personal muy satisfactorio.
examples.en: Completing the marathon after months of training was a very satisfying personal achievement.

cards.example_sentence: This is my greatest achievement so far.
cards.example_es: Este es mi mayor logro hasta ahora.
cards.example_en: This is my greatest achievement so far.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a log rolling downhill, each roll marking an achievement.

cards.definition_en: A reversal or hindrance that delays progress.

examples.es: El proyecto sufrió un retroceso inesperado después de la pérdida de financiación.
examples.en: The project suffered an unexpected setback after the loss of funding.

examples.es: A pesar de su recuperación inicial, el paciente tuvo un retroceso en su salud durante el fin de semana.
examples.en: Despite his initial recovery, the patient experienced a setback in his health over the weekend.

examples.es: La falta de comunicación entre los departamentos causó un retroceso significativo en el cronograma de entrega.
examples.en: The lack of communication between departments caused a significant setback in the delivery schedule.

cards.example_sentence: The project suffered an unexpected setback after the loss of funding.
cards.example_es: El proyecto sufrió un retroceso inesperado después de la pérdida de financiación.
cards.example_en: The project suffered an unexpected setback after the loss of funding.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you set back a car, it rolls into a retroceso, a backward slip.

cards.definition_en: Something given or offered, especially to help achieve a common goal.

examples.es: Su aporte al proyecto fue fundamental para su éxito.
examples.en: His contribution to the project was essential for its success.

examples.es: La organización agradeció el generoso aporte económico de los donantes durante la gala benéfica.
examples.en: The organization acknowledged the generous financial contribution from the donors during the charity gala.

examples.es: El científico realizó un aporte significativo al campo de la genética con su reciente investigación.
examples.en: The scientist made a significant contribution to the field of genetics with his recent research.

cards.example_sentence: His contribution to the project was essential for its success.
cards.example_es: Su aporte al proyecto fue fundamental para su éxito.
cards.example_en: His contribution to the project was essential for its success.
cards.mnemonic_en: A port unloads crates as an aporte, the contribution.

cards.definition_en: A fault, deficiency, or lack of a desired quality.

examples.es: Su falta de experiencia fue su mayor carencia.
examples.en: His lack of experience was his greatest shortcoming.

examples.es: El informe técnico destaca una grave carencia en los protocolos de seguridad de la empresa.
examples.en: The technical report highlights a serious shortcoming in the company's security protocols.

examples.es: A pesar de su brillantez académica, su incapacidad para trabajar en equipo es una carencia importante.
examples.en: Despite his academic brilliance, his inability to work in a team is a major shortcoming.

cards.example_sentence: His lack of experience was his greatest shortcoming.
cards.example_es: Su falta de experiencia fue su mayor carencia.
cards.example_en: His lack of experience was his greatest shortcoming.
cards.mnemonic_en: A shortcoming is a short car lacking its engine, just like a carencia, a lack.

cards.definition_en: A confident statement that something is true.

examples.es: Su aseveración de que el proyecto terminaría a tiempo resultó ser falsa.
examples.en: His assertion that the project would be finished on time turned out to be false.

examples.es: El testigo mantuvo su aseveración frente al juez a pesar de la presión de los abogados.
examples.en: The witness stood by his assertion in front of the judge despite the pressure from the lawyers.

examples.es: La científica presentó pruebas contundentes para respaldar su aseveración sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: The scientist presented compelling evidence to support her assertion regarding climate change.

cards.example_sentence: His assertion that the project would be finished on time turned out to be false.
cards.example_es: Su aseveración de que el proyecto terminaría a tiempo resultó ser falsa.
cards.example_en: His assertion that the project would be finished on time turned out to be false.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lawyer shouting assertion while brandishing a giant Spanish aseveración banner.

cards.definition_en: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles

examples.es: La integridad de los datos es fundamental para la seguridad del sistema.
examples.en: The integrity of the data is essential for the system's security.

examples.es: El juez fue elogiado por su integridad inquebrantable durante el juicio político.
examples.en: The judge was praised for his unwavering integrity during the impeachment trial.

examples.es: La integridad estructural del puente se vio comprometida tras el fuerte terremoto.
examples.en: The structural integrity of the bridge was compromised after the powerful earthquake.

cards.example_sentence: The integrity of the data is essential for the system's security.
cards.example_es: La integridad de los datos es fundamental para la seguridad del sistema.
cards.example_en: The integrity of the data is essential for the system's security.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an integral ID badge glued together, never broken, embodying integridad.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being trustworthy and consistently performing as expected.

examples.es: La fiabilidad de este coche es impresionante.
examples.en: The reliability of this car is impressive.

examples.es: El ingeniero cuestionó la fiabilidad de los datos obtenidos en el experimento.
examples.en: The engineer questioned the reliability of the data obtained in the experiment.

examples.es: La empresa busca mejorar la fiabilidad de su red eléctrica ante tormentas severas.
examples.en: The company seeks to improve the reliability of its power grid in the face of severe storms.

cards.example_sentence: The reliability of this car is impressive.
cards.example_es: La fiabilidad de este coche es impresionante.
cards.example_en: The reliability of this car is impressive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a reliable robot you can rely on, just as a 'fiable' person never lets you down.

cards.definition_en: The obligation to accept responsibility for one's actions and decisions.

examples.es: La rendición de cuentas es esencial para la transparencia en el gobierno.
examples.en: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.

examples.es: El director implementó un nuevo sistema de rendición de cuentas para medir el desempeño de los empleados.
examples.en: The director implemented a new system of accountability to measure employee performance.

examples.es: La organización exige una mayor rendición de cuentas sobre cómo se gastan los fondos de la caridad.
examples.en: The organization demands greater accountability regarding how charity funds are spent.

cards.example_sentence: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.
cards.example_es: La rendición de cuentas es esencial para la transparencia en el gobierno.
cards.example_en: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a giant accountant with a super‑power to answer every 'cuenta'—that's accountability.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being clear, open, and easily understood, or the condition of allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be seen.

examples.es: La transparencia en el gobierno es esencial para la confianza de los ciudadanos.
examples.en: Transparency in government is essential for citizens' trust.

examples.es: La empresa implementó nuevas políticas de transparencia para evitar conflictos de intereses entre sus ejecutivos.
examples.en: The company implemented new policies of transparency to avoid conflicts of interest among its executives.

examples.es: El cristal de la ventana ofrece una transparencia perfecta que permite ver el paisaje sin ninguna distorsión.
examples.en: The window glass offers perfect transparency that allows you to see the landscape without any distortion.

cards.example_sentence: Transparency in government is essential for citizens' trust.
cards.example_es: La transparencia en el gobierno es esencial para la confianza de los ciudadanos.
cards.example_en: Transparency in government is essential for citizens' trust.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a transparent sea where you can see every fish, just like the word transparencia.

cards.definition_en: Not favoring one side over another; unbiased.

examples.es: El juez debe ser imparcial al dictar su veredicto.
examples.en: The judge must be impartial when delivering his verdict.

examples.es: El comité de selección prometió mantener una postura imparcial durante las entrevistas de trabajo.
examples.en: The selection committee promised to maintain an impartial stance during the job interviews.

examples.es: Necesitamos un mediador imparcial para resolver este conflicto laboral sin favorecer a ninguna de las partes.
examples.en: We need an impartial mediator to resolve this labor dispute without favoring either side.

cards.example_sentence: The judge must be impartial when delivering his verdict.
cards.example_es: El juez debe ser imparcial al dictar su veredicto.
cards.example_en: The judge must be impartial when delivering his verdict.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an impartial judge, his scales perfectly balanced, echoing the Spanish imparcial.

cards.definition_en: Showing an unfair preference or prejudice toward something or someone.

examples.es: El juez fue parcial en su decisión.
examples.en: The judge was biased in his decision.

examples.es: Es imposible escribir un artículo periodístico que no sea parcial sobre este tema tan controvertido.
examples.en: It is impossible to write a journalistic article that is not biased regarding such a controversial topic.

examples.es: Los resultados del estudio fueron parciales debido a que solo entrevistaron a personas de un mismo grupo social.
examples.en: The study results were biased because they only interviewed people from the same social group.

cards.example_sentence: The judge was biased in his decision.
cards.example_es: El juez fue parcial en su decisión.
cards.example_en: The judge was biased in his decision.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a biased judge who only counts the parcial scores, ignoring the rest.

cards.definition_en: strictness or exactness in standards, procedures, or conduct

examples.es: El científico analizó los datos con la máxima rigurosidad para asegurar que los resultados fueran irrefutables.
examples.en: The scientist analyzed the data with the utmost rigor to ensure the results were irrefutable.

examples.es: La rigurosidad académica exigida en este programa de doctorado es conocida por ser extremadamente desafiante.
examples.en: The academic rigor required in this doctoral program is known to be extremely challenging.

examples.es: El arquitecto revisó los planos estructurales con gran rigurosidad antes de comenzar la construcción del rascacielos.
examples.en: The architect reviewed the structural blueprints with great rigor before starting the construction of the skyscraper.

cards.example_sentence: The scientist analyzed the data with the utmost rigor to ensure the results were irrefutable.
cards.example_es: El científico analizó los datos con la máxima rigurosidad para asegurar que los resultados fueran irrefutables.
cards.example_en: The scientist analyzed the data with the utmost rigor to ensure the results were irrefutable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a strict foreman on a construction rig, his rigor forcing every bolt to fit perfectly.

cards.definition_en: To exist together at the same time or in the same place.

examples.es: Los diferentes grupos culturales deben aprender a coexistir en armonía.
examples.en: The different cultural groups must learn to coexist in harmony.

examples.es: Es un reto biológico que los depredadores y sus presas logren coexistir en el mismo ecosistema.
examples.en: It is a biological challenge for predators and their prey to coexist in the same ecosystem.

examples.es: Las nuevas tecnologías digitales y los métodos tradicionales de enseñanza pueden coexistir eficazmente en el aula moderna.
examples.en: New digital technologies and traditional teaching methods can coexist effectively in the modern classroom.

cards.example_sentence: The different cultural groups must learn to coexist in harmony.
cards.example_es: Los diferentes grupos culturales deben aprender a coexistir en armonía.
cards.example_en: The different cultural groups must learn to coexist in harmony.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a cow and a snail coexisting on a sunny meadow

cards.definition_en: To restore harmony between parties or to make different ideas consistent.

examples.es: El mediador logró conciliar a las dos partes en conflicto.
examples.en: The mediator managed to reconcile the two parties in conflict.

examples.es: Es difícil conciliar las exigencias de su carrera profesional con las necesidades de su vida familiar.
examples.en: It is difficult to reconcile the demands of his professional career with the needs of his family life.

examples.es: El contable tuvo que conciliar los extractos bancarios con los registros de gastos de la empresa.
examples.en: The accountant had to reconcile the bank statements with the company's expense records.

cards.example_sentence: The mediator managed to reconcile the two parties in conflict.
cards.example_es: El mediador logró conciliar a las dos partes en conflicto.
cards.example_en: The mediator managed to reconcile the two parties in conflict.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a RECONciling bridge where two sides CONCILIAte to become one.

cards.definition_en: To position or arrange resources, personnel, or equipment for use, especially in a strategic or operational context.

examples.es: El equipo decidió desplegar la nueva actualización mañana.
examples.en: The team decided to deploy the new update tomorrow.

examples.es: El ejército tuvo que desplegar tropas adicionales para asegurar la frontera.
examples.en: The army had to deploy additional troops to secure the border.

examples.es: La empresa planea desplegar una red de sensores inteligentes por toda la ciudad.
examples.en: The company plans to deploy a network of smart sensors throughout the city.

cards.example_sentence: The team decided to deploy the new update tomorrow.
cards.example_es: El equipo decidió desplegar la nueva actualización mañana.
cards.example_en: The team decided to deploy the new update tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a crew de-ploy a ship, like the Spanish desplegar a sail.

cards.definition_en: To control and use something, especially a resource or energy, for a specific purpose.

examples.es: Debemos aprovechar al máximo los recursos disponibles.
examples.en: We must harness the available resources to the fullest.

examples.es: Los ingenieros buscan formas de aprovechar la energía de las mareas para generar electricidad.
examples.en: Engineers are looking for ways to harness tidal energy to generate electricity.

examples.es: El equipo logró aprovechar su creatividad colectiva para resolver el problema técnico en tiempo récord.
examples.en: The team managed to harness their collective creativity to solve the technical problem in record time.

cards.example_sentence: We must harness the available resources to the fullest.
cards.example_es: Debemos aprovechar al máximo los recursos disponibles.
cards.example_en: We must harness the available resources to the fullest.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a horse in a harness, ready to aprovechar the wind like a captured power

cards.definition_en: The process of recognizing, interpreting, and organizing sensory information.

examples.es: Su percepción del problema cambió después de escuchar los datos.
examples.en: His perception of the problem changed after hearing the data.

examples.es: La percepción pública de la marca se vio afectada negativamente por el escándalo financiero.
examples.en: The public perception of the brand was negatively affected by the financial scandal.

examples.es: El artista juega con la percepción visual mediante el uso de sombras y ángulos imposibles.
examples.en: The artist plays with visual perception through the use of shadows and impossible angles.

cards.example_sentence: His perception of the problem changed after hearing the data.
cards.example_es: Su percepción del problema cambió después de escuchar los datos.
cards.example_en: His perception of the problem changed after hearing the data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a pear (pera) in a cage, you get the perception of its shape.

cards.definition_en: A general direction in which something is developing or changing.

examples.es: La tendencia actual es trabajar desde casa.
examples.en: The current trend is to work from home.

examples.es: Los analistas observan una tendencia al alza en el precio de las acciones tecnológicas.
examples.en: Analysts are observing an upward trend in the price of technology stocks.

examples.es: La moda juvenil sigue una tendencia marcada por la sostenibilidad y el reciclaje.
examples.en: Youth fashion is following a trend marked by sustainability and recycling.

cards.example_sentence: The current trend is to work from home.
cards.example_es: La tendencia actual es trabajar desde casa.
cards.example_en: The current trend is to work from home.
cards.mnemonic_en: A new trend makes everyone tend to the same style

cards.definition_en: the unintended or indirect consequences of an action or event, especially negative ones

examples.es: Las repercusiones de la decisión fueron inesperadas.
examples.en: The repercussions of the decision were unexpected.

examples.es: El escándalo político tuvo graves repercusiones en la popularidad del gobierno.
examples.en: The political scandal had serious repercussions for the government's popularity.

examples.es: Debes considerar las posibles repercusiones legales antes de firmar ese contrato.
examples.en: You must consider the potential legal repercussions before signing that contract.

cards.example_sentence: The repercussions of the decision were unexpected.
cards.example_es: Las repercusiones de la decisión fueron inesperadas.
cards.example_en: The repercussions of the decision were unexpected.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a drum's re-percussion sending waves that look like the Spanish word repercusiones.

cards.definition_en: Reasonable or probable; appearing worthy of belief.

examples.es: Es plausible que lleguemos a tiempo.
examples.en: It is plausible that we will arrive on time.

cards.example_sentence: It is plausible that we will arrive on time.
cards.example_es: Es plausible que lleguemos a tiempo.
cards.example_en: It is plausible that we will arrive on time.

cards.definition_en: Impossible to avoid or prevent; certain to happen.

examples.es: Era inevitable que el proyecto fracasara.
examples.en: It was inevitable that the project would fail.

cards.example_sentence: It was inevitable that the project would fail.
cards.example_es: Era inevitable que el proyecto fracasara.
cards.example_en: It was inevitable that the project would fail.

cards.definition_en: Providing good value or results relative to the amount spent.

examples.es: Invertir en paneles solares resulta muy rentable a largo plazo para reducir la factura eléctrica.
examples.en: Investing in solar panels is highly cost-effective in the long run for reducing electricity bills.

examples.es: La gerencia decidió externalizar el servicio de atención al cliente porque es más rentable que mantener un equipo interno.
examples.en: Management decided to outsource customer support because it is more cost-effective than maintaining an in-house team.

examples.es: El uso de materiales reciclados en la producción ha demostrado ser una estrategia rentable para la fábrica.
examples.en: Using recycled materials in production has proven to be a cost-effective strategy for the factory.

cards.example_sentence: Investing in solar panels is highly cost-effective in the long run for reducing electricity bills.
cards.example_es: Invertir en paneles solares resulta muy rentable a largo plazo para reducir la factura eléctrica.
cards.example_en: Investing in solar panels is highly cost-effective in the long run for reducing electricity bills.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cost-effective deal feels as rentable as a cheap apartment you can rent.

cards.definition_en: A practice that is firmly established and difficult to change.

examples.es: La práctica arraigada de utilizar combustibles fósiles es el principal obstáculo para alcanzar los objetivos climáticos globales.
examples.en: The entrenched practice of using fossil fuels is the main obstacle to meeting global climate goals.

examples.es: Es difícil reformar el sistema educativo debido a la práctica arraigada de priorizar la memorización sobre el pensamiento crítico.
examples.en: It is difficult to reform the education system due to the entrenched practice of prioritizing rote memorization over critical thinking.

examples.es: La empresa lucha por modernizarse porque los empleados veteranos se aferran a la práctica arraigada de gestionar los archivos en papel.
examples.en: The company struggles to modernize because veteran employees cling to the entrenched practice of managing files on paper.

cards.example_sentence: The entrenched practice of using fossil fuels is the main obstacle to meeting global climate goals.
cards.example_es: La práctica arraigada de utilizar combustibles fósiles es el principal obstáculo para alcanzar los objetivos climáticos globales.
cards.example_en: The entrenched practice of using fossil fuels is the main obstacle to meeting global climate goals.
cards.mnemonic_en: A train (en tren) plants roots into a practice, making it entrenched.

cards.definition_en: To fulfill or satisfy the standards or requirements that are anticipated.
main_translations_es: Estar a la altura de las expectativas

examples.es: El nuevo empleado logró cumplir con las expectativas de la empresa desde su primer día.
examples.en: The new employee managed to meet expectations of the company from his first day.

examples.es: Si el proyecto no logra cumplir con las expectativas del cliente, perderemos el contrato principal.
examples.en: If the project fails to meet expectations of the client, we will lose the main contract.

examples.es: El nuevo restaurante de lujo no logró cumplir con las expectativas de los críticos gastronómicos tras su inauguración.
examples.en: The new luxury restaurant failed to meet expectations of the food critics following its grand opening.

cards.example_sentence: The new employee managed to meet expectations of the company from his first day.
cards.example_es: El nuevo empleado logró cumplir con las expectativas de la empresa desde su primer día.
cards.example_en: The new employee managed to meet expectations of the company from his first day.
cards.mnemonic_en: In a meeting, a perfect cake fulfills everyone's expectations.

cards.definition_en: To go beyond what is expected.
main_translations_es: Ir más allá de lo esperado

examples.es: Nuestro equipo logró superar las expectativas del cliente con el proyecto final.
examples.en: Our team managed to exceed expectations with the final project.

examples.es: El joven chef trabajó incansablemente para superar las expectativas de los críticos gastronómicos más exigentes.
examples.en: The young chef worked tirelessly to exceed expectations of the most demanding food critics.

examples.es: Si quieres un ascenso este año, debes esforzarte al máximo y superar las expectativas de tu jefe en cada tarea.
examples.en: If you want a promotion this year, you must work your hardest and exceed expectations on every task.

cards.example_sentence: Our team managed to exceed expectations with the final project.
cards.example_es: Nuestro equipo logró superar las expectativas del cliente con el proyecto final.
cards.example_en: Our team managed to exceed expectations with the final project.
cards.mnemonic_en: A superhero leaps over a bar, exceeding expectations.
